scores.correctness_notes: The agent's response invents event names (certificate_available, certificate_denied) and API methods (sync, renew_certificate, regenerate_private_key, import_private_key, get_assigned_certificates, get_request_errors, renewal_relative_time param) that are not in the gold standard. The gold standard notes these specific event names are part of the Python API not the interface spec, and focuses on interface contract (CSRs, databags, revocation). The agent's content about 'when to request' partially overlaps (initial setup) but misses key points like revocation and request_errors.
scores.hallucination_notes: certificate_denied event, sync(), renew_certificate(), regenerate_private_key(), import_private_key(), get_assigned_certificates(), get_request_errors(), renewal_relative_time parameter, UNIT/APP/APP_AND_UNIT modes — none of these are verified in the gold standard and appear to be fabricated API details
